rationale.D1: YAGSL provides a vendored swerve abstraction but all mechanism subsystems (ShooterSubsystem, HoodSubsystem, IntakePivotSubsystem, ConveyorSubsystem, KickerSubsystem, ClimberSubsystem) hold SparkFlex motors directly with no IO interface or swappable implementations; no 'interface *IO' found anywhere.
rationale.D2: RobotContainer uses command composition (SequentialCommandGroup, ParallelCommandGroup, ConditionalCommand) and Trigger-based auto-firing (isReadyToShoot, teleJammedForward) with NamedCommands for auto, going beyond simple button bindings but with no Superstructure coordinator, no explicit state enum transitions, and no goal-request API.
rationale.D3: WPILib physics simulation (FlywheelSim, ElevatorSim, SingleJointedArmSim) wired into every main mechanism subsystem via simulationPeriodic, plus VisionSystemSim with four PhotonCameraSim instances; swerve simulationPeriodic is an empty stub, scoring this D3=2 not 3.
rationale.D4: No src/test directory exists and no test files were found anywhere in the 2026_Rebuilt repo; D4=0.
rationale.D5: Logging is SmartDashboard.put* scattered through subsystems (confirmed in ShooterSubsystem, SwerveSubsystem, Vision, HoodSubsystem, etc.); no AdvantageKit, DogLog, or Epilogue found in vendordeps or source.
rationale.D6: AutoBuilder with PathPlanner configured with real constraints and 20+ named autos confirmed in RobotContainer.setupAuto(); Choreo .traj files are actively used via 'choreoAuto: true' in all .auto files (27 autos confirmed), and PathfindToPose is used for on-the-fly pose navigation.
rationale.D7: Four PhotonVision cameras with PhotonPoseEstimator (MULTI_TAG_PNP_ON_COPROCESSOR), dynamic std dev scaling by distance and tag count, rejection logic (minDist thresholds, disconnect filter, manual disable), and addVisionMeasurement wired into SwerveDrive; no TimeInterpolatableBuffer or dedicated RobotState class.
rationale.D8: Five-plus seasons of public repos (2022-2026), a maintained RobotTools series (educational site with tutorials in RobotTools2026) providing onboarding resources, but no GitHub Actions CI, no spotless/checkstyle, and no separate maintained library package.
